text: I arrive as seed-stage founder evaluating whether Betabots can replace early beta feedback. My past: I have no patience for fake AI praise because product mistakes cost runway.
My screen is Laptop 15 (1440x900, desktop).
Today I want to: See real evidence, understand what a run produces, and decide whether to request a review.
My life goal is: Protect my company, runway, and reputation by choosing feedback systems that reveal truth early.
I have 100 life-years. Each meaningful action costs 1 month(s); each committed dollar costs 1 year(s).

label: arrival
title: Betabots - Synthetic beta users with real product evidence
visibleText: Docs Contribute Betabots Cloud View Beta Run locally OPEN SOURCE Local-first Launch with a room full of ruthless beta users. Betabots runs truthful synthetic beta users in real browser sessions. Bots click, wait, think, doubt, compare notes, and leave inspectable evidence you can trust. I think: I am interested, but I do not trust the promise yet. The page says inspectable evidence, and that is exactly what I need to see.
My reaction: My reaction is guarded curiosity. The positioning is strong, but I need to see whether the reviews are blunt or just polished summaries.
Idea: Show one full sample run immediately, with the raw actions, doubts, and final review visible before asking me to request anything.
Life-cost decision: Spending 1 month of life is worth it if opening the public demo gives me real evidence before I risk my company, runway, or reputation.
Truth assessment: My private judgment is that this could be useful for a founder, but only if the demo proves the bots catch uncomfortable issues. Confidence: medium.
My impulse is to explore.
I think: I feel pulled toward doing something concrete rather than browsing forever.
I follow a hunch and check /demo.

label: destiny /demo
title: Betabots Demo Dashboard | Betabots
visibleText: Back to landing PUBLIC BETA DASHBOARD Three sample product reviews from cloud Betabots. Each review shows what a cohort noticed, what actions they took, and which findings would change a product decision. 162 cloud Betabots simulated across the sample pack GitHub github.com 82 Strong developer trust, mild onboarding sprawl. 60 bots 2-6 min sessions high-signal findings Top findings Open-source credibility is immediate, but new contributors split between docs, issues, and navigation. Bots trusted repository history faster than marketing claims. Mobile exploration stayed readable, but advanced flows became dense quickly. Observed actions Opened repository Read README Compared issues Checked co

I think: I see a clearer promise now: not generic praise, but inspectable runs with actual user-like behavior and review artifacts.
My reaction: I am still guarded, but the public demo and named sample reviews make this feel more concrete than most AI-feedback pitches.
Idea: Put one strong sample review directly on this page with screenshots, quotes, and the exact decisions it changed.
Life-cost decision: Spending 1 more month of life is justified if opening the demo shows whether this can actually protect my runway and reputation.
Truth assessment: My private judgment is that Betabots might be useful, but I need to inspect the public demo before trusting it; confidence 0.68.
My impulse is to explore.
